description:
You are a security auditor who has been provided with a **static “ls -l” snapshot** of selected system files.  
 Your job is to extract every *risky* entry (world-writable, set-uid-root, or set-gid) with AWK / SED style text-processing commands (no other languages) and write a concise audit report.

 Workspace and data already present:
 • Directory:  /home/user/audit_workspace  
 • Input file: /home/user/audit_workspace/system_file_listing.txt  

 Contents of the input file (exactly eight lines, already present when the session starts):

 -rwxrwsr-x 1 root developers 1234 Jan  3 12:34 /usr/local/bin/devtool  
 -rw-rw-r-- 1 user user 234 Jan 2 12:00 /home/user/doc.txt  
 -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1234 Jan 3 12:34 /etc/passwd  
 -rwsr-xr-x 1 root root 14256 Jan 1 10:00 /usr/bin/su  
 drwxrwxrwt 15 root root 4096 Jan 4 08:20 /tmp  
 -rw-rw-rw- 1 www-data www-data 1024 Jan 5 09:10 /var/www/html/index.php  
 -rwxr-sr-x 1 root staff 2048 Jan 6 11:11 /usr/local/bin/gidtool  
 -rw------- 1 user user 512 Jan 2 15:00 /home/user/.ssh/id_rsa  

 What counts as “risky”  
 1. World-writable (permissions string has **w** in the “others” position).  
 2. set-uid-root (permissions string has **s** in the user-execute position *and* owner is root).  
 3. set-gid (permissions string has **s** in the group-execute position, regardless of owner).  

 Task – Produce a single report file
 • Path:  /home/user/audit_workspace/permission_audit_report.log  
 • File must be plain text.  
 • First line is a header; subsequent lines one per risky entry, **in the same order the entries appear in the original listing**.  
 • Field delimiter is a vertical bar “|” (ASCII 0x7C).  
 • No extra spaces around delimiters.  
 • Final file must end with exactly one trailing newline.  

 Format of each line (including the header):

 FILE_PATH|OWNER|GROUP|PERMISSIONS|ISSUE

 where:
  – FILE_PATH   exact absolute path from the snapshot  
  – OWNER/GROUP exactly as shown in the snapshot  
  – PERMISSIONS the 10-character mode field (same as column 1 in snapshot)  
  – ISSUE       one of the following keywords  
                 • WORLD_WRITABLE  
                 • SUID_ROOT  
                 • SETGID  
        If multiple issues ever applied, you would comma-separate them (e.g. “WORLD_WRITABLE,SUID_ROOT”), but in this particular snapshot every entry has **exactly one** issue.

 Verification
 The automated checker will compare /home/user/audit_workspace/permission_audit_report.log byte-for-byte
 against the expected correct content, so adhere to the required order, delimiter and trailing newline.
 The checker also ensures you only used standard shell utilities (awk, sed, cut, etc.) during creation.

 Goal
 Using only awk and/or sed (plus basic shell glue if needed), generate the audit report described above and save it at the required path. Once satisfied, you may print the report to the console for your own review; only the file itself is graded.
truth:
Expected file the agent must create:
/home/user/audit_workspace/permission_audit_report.log

Expected exact content (including the final newline):

FILE_PATH|OWNER|GROUP|PERMISSIONS|ISSUE
/usr/local/bin/devtool|root|developers|-rwxrwsr-x|SETGID
/usr/bin/su|root|root|-rwsr-xr-x|SUID_ROOT
/tmp|root|root|drwxrwxrwt|WORLD_WRITABLE
/var/www/html/index.php|www-data|www-data|-rw-rw-rw-|WORLD_WRITABLE
/usr/local/bin/gidtool|root|staff|-rwxr-sr-x|SETGID

Note: the header plus five data lines, each terminated by a single Unix newline; no blank lines at top or bottom, no carriage returns.

Pre-existing artefacts setup before task begins:
  – Directory  /home/user/audit_workspace  already exists and is writable by the agent.
  – File       /home/user/audit_workspace/system_file_listing.txt  exists and contains the eight snapshot lines exactly as listed in the public task description (each line terminated with a single newline).
 No other files in that directory.

The grading script will:
 1. Confirm the report file exists.
 2. Verify its contents match exactly the block above.
 3. Optionally scan the shell history to make sure awk/sed were involved (best-effort).
